mnemonics: IF ID EX MEM WB - I Don't EXpect MEMory to Work Backwards (Instruction Fetch, Instruction Decode, Execute, Memory, Write Back)
Hazards are RAW, WAR, WAW - Read After Write, Write After Read, Write After Write. Remember: 'Real Architects Worry'
keyPoints: Pipelining improves throughput by allowing multiple instructions to be in different stages of execution simultaneously.
The maximum speedup is limited by the slowest stage (the 'bottleneck') and the number of pipeline stages.
Hazards (Structural, Data, Control) disrupt the pipeline flow and require stalling or forwarding to resolve.
commonMistakes: Assuming pipelining decreases the execution time of a single instruction (latency); it only improves total throughput.
Forgetting that control hazards from branches are a major performance limiter and that branch prediction is a key optimization.
Confusing data hazards (RAW, WAR, WAW) and their solutions (e.g., using forwarding for RAW hazards instead of just stalling).
